data.title: Cushman & Wakefield beats Q4 topline estimates
data.description:
• Cushman & Wakefield press release (CWK): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 in-line.
  • Revenue of $2.9B (+11.5% Y/Y) beats by $70M.
  • Net loss was $22.4 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to net income of $112.9 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: N-able Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 misses by $0.04, revenue of $130.3M beats by $3.23M
data.description:
• N-able press release (NABL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $130.3M (+11.8% Y/Y) beats by $3.23M.
  • Subscription revenue of $129.0 million, representing 12.1% year-over-year growth, or 9.3% year-over-year growth on a constant currency basis.
  • Total ARR of $539.7 million, representing 11.9% year-over-year growth, or 7.7% year-over-year growth on a constant currency basis.
  • GAAP gross margin of 76.2% and non-GAAP gross margin of 79.8%.

data.title: Walmart Non-GAAP EPS of $0.74 beats by $0.01, revenue of $190.7B beats by $2.38B
data.description:
• Walmart press release (WMT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.74 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $190.7B (+5.6% Y/Y) beats by $2.38B.
  • Global eCommerce sales grew 24%, led by store-fulfilled pickup & delivery and marketplace
  • Global advertising business up 37%, including VIZIO; Walmart Connect in the U.S. up 41%
  • Membership fee revenue grew 15.1% globally
  •  Lookingahead, the Company issues guidance for FY27 with netsales expected to grow 3.5% to 4.5% vs 5.01% consensus, and adjustedoperating income to grow 6.0% to 8.0%, both in constantcurrency (“cc”). Adjusted EPS is expected to be $2.75 to$2.85 vs $2.97 consensus
  • The Company’s first quarter fiscal 2027 guidance is based on the following Q1 FY26 figures: Net sales:$164.0 billion vs 5.42% consensus growth in revenue, operating income: $7.1 billion, and adjusted EPS:$0.61 vs $0.68 consensus
  • Shares -1.5% PM.

data.title: Yeti Holdings falls as investors eye full-year guidance, tariff impact
data.description:
Yeti Holdings (YETI) turned lower in premarket trading on Thursday after setting profit guidance below expectations.

Revenue was up 6.8% year-over-year to $583.7M, driven by 25% international net sales growth. Drinkware net sales grew 6% during the quarter. Direct-to-consumer channel sales increased 7% to $394.3M, due to growth across all DTC businesses, including the Amazon Marketplace, corporate sales, YETI retail stores, and YETI websites. Wholesale channel sales increased 6% to $189.4M, primarily due to growth in international regions. 

Adjusted gross profit increased 2% to $340.9 million, or 58.4% of adjusted sales. The 180 basis point decrease in adjusted gross margin included a 310 basis point unfavorable impact from higher tariff costs, partially offset by lower product costs and selective price increases implemented in Q2 of 2025. Adjusted net income per diluted share decreased 8% to $0.92 vs. $0.88 consensus. Adjusted net income per diluted share in the current quarter included an unfavorable net impact from higher tariff costs of approximately $0.15.

Looking ahead, Yeti Holdings (YETI) sees full-year adjusted EPS of $2.77 to $2.83 (midpoint $2.80) vs. the consensus estimate of $2.92. "We expect full-year sales growth between 6% and 8%, supported by balanced contributions from both Drinkware and Coolers & Equipment, continued international strength, and a robust innovation cycle," highlighted CEO Matt Reintjes. "When we look at our long-range plan and the progress we’re making across geographies and product categories, we see an even broader runway and a clear path to high-single to low-double digit growth," he added. The company also expects capital expenditures between $60M and $70M, primarily to support investments in technology, new product innovation, and the supply chain. Free cash flow is expected to land in a range of $200M and $225M for the year.

On Wall Street, Jefferies analyst Randal Konik noted that the quarter showed that the product and geographic expansion are still humming along. "Tariffs still a thorn, but supply chain being managed and EPS back to DD%," highlighted Konik. The firm kept a Buy rating on Yeit (YETI) in place.

Shares of Yeti Holdings (YETI) were down 6.4% in premarket action.

data.title: Hims & Hers to buy Australian digital health firm Eucalyptus for over $1B
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) added ~7% in the premarket on Thursday after announcing an agreement to acquire Eucalyptus, an Australian digital health provider, in a cash and stock transaction valued at up to $1.15B.

With an annual revenue run rate exceeding $450M, Sydney-based Eucalyptus is Australia's largest digital health provider. Hims (HIMS) expects the acquisition to help it expand in Australia and Japan while boosting its footprint in the UK, Germany, and Canada.

Under the deal, Hims (HIMS) is required to pay nearly $240M in cash after closing the transaction. Eucalyptus is eligible to receive deferred earnout payments over the subsequent 18 months, and additional payments are also due until early 2029, depending on the achievement of financial milestones.

The company has the option to pay the majority of deferred and earnout payments in cash or stock.

San Francisco, California-based Hims (HIMS), which markets compounded versions of weight loss drugs, expects to finance the deal mostly with cash on hand and operating cash flows tied to its U.S. operations. The transaction is expected to close in mid-2026.

data.title: Domino's Pizza is a growing favorite of Berkshire Hathaway
data.description:
Domino's Pizza (DPZ) continues to be a favorite investment of Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) after the Omaha-based company added to its position again in the fourth quarter. The latest 13F filing from Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) showed a roughly 9.9% economic stake worth a bit over 1% of the total portfolio. 

Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) first initiated its position in Domino's (DPZ) in Q3 of 2024, buying about 1.3M shares at an average price in the mid-$430s to create a new billion-dollar-class position. By the end of Q4 of 2024, the stake was lifted to roughly 2.4M shares, signaling that this was a high-conviction consumer-franchise bet rather than a small "toe-dip" type of trade.

During the first half of 2025, Berkshire continued to add to the Domino's (DPZ) position, buying roughly 239K shares in Q1 of 2025 and then more shares in subsequent quarters, even as it trimmed larger holdings like Apple (AAPL) to fund new and expanded positions. By the end of Q2 of 2025, the position size was around 2.6M shares, representing under 1% of the reported equity portfolio but already over 7% of Domino's (DPZ) outstanding shares.

The most recent disclosure showed Berkshire (BRK.A) (BRK.B) buying Domino’s for the sixth straight quarter into 4Q25, and amassing about 3.35M total shares. The 9.9% stake in the restaurant operator is just below the 10% regulatory threshold that can trigger additional reporting obligations. 

Analysts highlighted that Domino's (DPZ) global scale, asset-light franchising model, and aggressive capital returns are attributes that appeal to the Warren Buffett model.

data.title: TechnipFMC Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 beats by $0.18, revenue of $2.52B misses by $20M
data.description:
• TechnipFMC press release (FTI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 beats by $0.18.
